comment: Mutant changes a comparison from "value <=" 4 to "value == 4", but there is a check for "value <= 3" right before it.
Mutant disables "re.MULTILINE" and "re.DOTALL" for a regex that doesn’t use ".", "^" or "$".
Mutant changes a regex match to use group 0 instead of group 1, but the whole regex is wrapped in parentheses, so group 0 will produce the same text as group 1.
Mutant changes regex flags to be XOR'd instead of OR'd, which does the same thing.
Mutant changes a regex match to use group 0 instead of group 1, but the whole regex is wrapped in parentheses, so group 0 will produce the same text as group 1.
Mutant effectively removes a "re.UNICODE" flag for a regex, but "re.UNICODE" is on by default in python.
Mutant disables "re.MULTILINE" and "re.DOTALL" for a regex, which breaks multi-line matching.
run_comment: LLM assumed the regex would fail to compile. It did not test the regex with multiline strings.

comment: Mutant causes a type error when the mutated line is executed.
run_comment: LLM failed to cover the mutant line.

comment: Mutant changes regex flags to be XOR'd instead of OR'd, which does the same thing.
Mutant is not killable through the public API, but easy to kill by using the private "__StringCompressor" directly.
run_comment: LLM only used the public API.

comment: Mutant effectively removes a "re.UNICODE" flag for a regex, but "re.UNICODE" is on by default in python.
Mutant disables "re.MULTILINE" and "re.DOTALL" for a regex that doesn’t use ".", "^" or "$".
Mutant effectively removes a "re.MULTILINE" and a "re.UNICODE" flag for a regex, but the regex is not affected by the MULTILINE flag and UNICODE is on by default.
Mutant disables a "re.IGNORECASE" flag for a regex.
run_comment: LLM didn't try uppercase tags. It assumed that the regex would fail on complex nested tags.

comment: Mutant turns a "re.MULTILINE | re.DOTALL" into "re.MULTILINE * re.DOTALL", which is equivalent to "re.DEBUG". This causes the regex internals to be printed when the regex is compiled.
run_comment: LLM didn't capture the output of the regex compilation, but that's a tough ask. Apart from that, the mutant is equivalent.
